title: verbascum phlomoides subsp. australe
intro: Verbascum phlomoides subsp. australae is a flowering plant, also known as the woolly mullein, which belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Verbascum phlomoides subsp. australae include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic agent. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and salves, can be used to treat a range of health issues, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
